content: Sebastien signed off on no-meeting Tuesdays pilot in standup. Ari and Anya both nodded; Marcus looked skeptical. Plan: full enforcement starting next week, track calendar blocks in Linear.
No-meeting Tuesdays rationale: async Slack + Linear reduce context-switching. Target: 90min uninterrupted deep work minimum per person. Measure via Slack activity delta and engineer velocity.
summary: No-meeting Tuesdays core hypothesis and metrics

content: First Tuesday without meetings. Jenny posted 3 design iterations by 11am. Devon shipped backend refactor. Tom asked in Slack why no standup—reminded him it's async-only Tuesdays.
summary: First no-meeting Tuesday shows early velocity gains

content: Process for no-meeting Tuesdays enforcement: (1) Maya blocks everyone's calendar at 12:01am Monday. (2) Anya auto-declines conflicting invites. (3) Slack reminder pinned in #engineering. (4) Retro every 2 weeks to flag violations.
summary: Enforcement procedure: blocking, auto-decline, reminders, retro

content: Q4 results: no-meeting Tuesdays reduced avg Slack interrupts by 34%. Sprint velocity +12%. Three weeks of clean data. Ari reported engineers felt noticeably less fragmented.
summary: Q4 no-meeting Tuesdays metrics: -34% interrupts, +12% velocity

content: Retro on Dec 15: Lina complained Tuesdays blocked product sync with Acme. Anya noted finance needed cross-team alignment. Consensus: worth the friction, but watch Q1 pressures.
summary: Q4 retro: friction points flagged but net positive

content: SOC2 audit kicked off Jan 5. Required daily sync calls with Anya, Priya, Devon, Marcus. Compliance timeline non-negotiable: certification needed by March for Globex contract.
summary: SOC2 audit drives daily syncs Jan-Mar, Globex contract dependent

content: Jan 14 standup: Sebastien asked if no-meeting Tuesdays still active. Maya admitted Tuesdays have become 'soft guidelines.' Three blocked meetings last week alone for SOC2. Sebastien nodded sympathetically.
summary: SOC2 demands erode no-meeting Tuesday enforcement in Jan

content: Q1 pattern: no-meeting Tuesday experiment stalled. Compliance pressure + Initech escalation (contract renewal) + James onboarding created meeting bloat. Tuesdays regressed to 60% adherence by mid-Jan.
summary: Q1 stall: SOC2, Initech, onboarding break no-meeting Tuesday

content: Maya's frustration: experiments need protection from operational chaos or they die. Can't half-enforce. Either commit with hard rules + sponsor cover, or admit defeat upfront. This ambiguity wastes everyone's hope.
summary: Maya on failed half-measures: experiments need real protection

content: Feb 3 1:1 with Sebastien: Maya pitched resurrection plan. SOC2 daily syncs drop to 3/week starting Feb 10. Globex timeline clarified: cert due Mar 15, not end-March. Window exists to restore Tuesdays.
summary: Feb 1:1: SOC2 timeline loosens, revival window opens

content: Revised no-meeting Tuesday enforcement (v2): hard calendar blocks + Anya's auto-decline now applies to founders too. Slack #no-meeting-tuesdays channel for async decision-making. Violations logged publicly weekly.
summary: v2 enforcement: founder blocks, public logging, async channel

content: Feb 18 retro: first Tuesday in 3 weeks with real adherence. Jenny shipped design system. Devon fixed Sentry alerts without meetings. Tom asked zero questions in standup because context was async. Clear win.
summary: Week 1 revival shows immediate productivity recovery

content: Decision doc filed Feb 26: no-meeting Tuesdays permanent, strong enforcement. Hard blocks for all staff + founders. Async-first for Tuesday decisions. SOC2 syncs rerouted to Mon/Wed/Thu. Globex contract enables this stability.
summary: Feb 26 decision: permanent no-meeting Tuesdays, enforced

content: Feb 25 Slack: Ari wrote 'Tuesday feeling like Tuesday again.' Marcus didn't complain about customer context-switching. Anya approved calendar automation spend ($2.4k Outlook integration). Momentum feels real.
summary: Team sentiment positive; automation investment approved

content: No-meeting Tuesday lesson: protection requires sponsor cover + external constraint removal. Q4 worked because no hard deadlines. Q1 failed because SOC2 was immovable. Feb works because Globex timeline clarified priorities.
summary: Lesson: experiments need sponsor + constraint removal to survive
